report_name: Aphena Pharma Hit by Chaos Ransomware; 142 GB of Data Claimed Stolen

threat_actors.name: Chaos
threat_actors.description: The Chaos ransomware group claimed an attack on Aphena Pharma Solutions, listing the company on its dark web leak site and alleging the theft of 142 GB of corporate data. The group is known for targeting mid-sized organizations and employing a double-extortion strategy.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through data encryption and the threat of leaking stolen sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Chaos ransomware payload was deployed to encrypt files on Aphena Pharma's network, as part of a double-extortion attack requiring a ransom to decrypt the files.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The Chaos group claimed to have exfiltrated 142 GB of sensitive corporate data from Aphena Pharma's network before deploying the ransomware payload.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups like Chaos frequently delete Volume Shadow Copies using commands such as 'vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet' to prevent easy restoration of encrypted files.

malware.name: Chaos Ransomware
malware.description: A ransomware payload used by the Chaos group to encrypt files on the victim's network as part of a double-extortion scheme. It is associated with file extensions like .chaos.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Mentioned as a tool used by ransomware groups like Chaos for lateral movement within a compromised network.

tools.name: rclone
tools.description: Mentioned as a tool used by ransomware groups like Chaos for exfiltrating large amounts of data to cloud storage.

tools.name: megacmd
tools.description: Mentioned as a tool used by ransomware groups like Chaos for exfiltrating data.

identities.name: Aphena Pharma Solutions
identities.description: A U.S.-based pharmaceutical contract manufacturer and packager targeted by the Chaos ransomware group, who claimed to have stolen 142 GB of data.

infrastructure.name: Chaos Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Chaos ransomware group to publish names of their victims and threaten to leak stolen data.

infrastructure.name: Chaos Exfiltration Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Cloud infrastructure used by the Chaos group to receive the 142 GB of data allegedly exfiltrated from Aphena Pharma.

indicators.name: Chaos Group Associated Tool Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Chaos Group Associated Tool Execution
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e5f-4a3b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of tools like PsExec, rclone, or megacmd, which are commonly used by ransomware groups like Chaos for lateral movement and data exfiltration.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-16
references:
  - https://galaxywarden.com/blog/breach/aphenapharma-com-chaos-2026-07
tags:
  - attack.t1570
  - attack.t1048
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\PsExec.exe'
      - '\rclone.exe'
      - '\megacmd.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of tools commonly used by the Chaos ransomware group for lateral movement and data exfiltration.

indicators.name: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via VSSAdmin
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via VSSAdmin
id: f8a1b2c3-d4e5-4f6a-b7c8-d9e0f1a2b3c4
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-16
references:
  - https://galaxywarden.com/blog/breach/aphenapharma-com-chaos-2026-07
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects attempts to delete Volume Shadow Copies using vssadmin.exe, a technique frequently used by ransomware like Chaos to prevent file restoration.

indicators.name: Chaos Ransomware File Extensions
indicators.pattern:
title: Chaos Ransomware File Extensions Detected
id: 9c8d7e6f-5a4b-4c3d-b2a1-e0f9e8d7c6b5
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with extensions commonly used by Chaos ransomware, such as .chaos or .locked.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-16
references:
  - https://galaxywarden.com/blog/breach/aphenapharma-com-chaos-2026-07
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.chaos'
      - '.locked'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files being renamed with extensions like .chaos or .locked, which are associated with the Chaos ransomware family.

relationships.source_name: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via VSSAdmin
